Title: Nutrient regulation of lipochitooligosaccharide recognition in plants via NSP1 and NSP2

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates common symbiosis signaling and promotes arbuscular mycorrhizal fungal colonization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for nutrient starvation-enabled LCO perception and contributes to AMF colonization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLK10 expression in barley is induced by nutrient starvation. 2. Its induction depends on NSP1/NSP2 and D14L signaling. 3. 5-deoxystrigol and karrikins promote RLK10 expression under repressive phosphate conditions. 4. rlk2/rlk10 double mutants lose LCO-induced calcium spiking and show reduced AMF colonization, supporting RLK10 as an LCO receptor.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; double mutant phenotyping; calcium oscillation assays; RT-qPCR; AMF colonization assays; hormone pretreatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in roots under N and/or P starvation, especially nutrient-deficient conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root/lateral root expression induced under nutrient starvation and by NSP2 overexpression
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen starvation; phosphorus starvation; 5-deoxystrigol treatment; karrikin treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single rlk10 mutants showed no significant AMF colonization defect, but rlk2/rlk10 double mutants were significantly impaired, indicating partial redundancy with RLK2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance cereal responsiveness to beneficial fungal LCO signals under nutrient-poor or phosphate-replete agricultural conditions, especially in combination with RLK2 and NSP2 engineering.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates common symbiosis signaling and promotes arbuscular mycorrhizal fungal colonization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to both LCO and CO perception and, together with RLK10, supports AMF colonization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLK2 is upregulated under nutrient starvation. 2. rlk2 single mutants show attenuated LCO- and CO-induced calcium oscillations. 3. rlk2/rlk10 double mutants completely lose LCO responsiveness and have reduced AMF colonization. 4. RLK2 acts redundantly with RLK10 in nutrient-dependent symbiotic signal perception.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutant analysis; calcium oscillation assays; AMF colonization assays; expression analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in roots under nutrient starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression increased under nutrient starvation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RLK2 contributes more strongly than RLK10 to CO perception; RLK10 appears the nutrient-regulated LCO receptor emphasized by the paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate co-engineering target with RLK10 for boosting cereal symbiotic signal perception.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves carotenoid intermediates to generate apocarotenoid precursors used for strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulate AMF hyphal branching and fungal LCO production, promoting mycorrhization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CCD7 reduces AMF colonization, indicating strigolactone biosynthesis is required for AMF association
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ccd7 mutants in M. truncatula show strong reductions in R. irregularis colonization. 2. This supports the requirement of strigolactone biosynthesis for AMF symbiosis. 3. The positive effect is attributed to rhizospheric signaling rather than plant D14-mediated perception.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tnt1 mutant analysis; AMF colonization assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although important for AMF colonization, CCD7 is not a principal experimental focus compared with NSP1/NSP2.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ccd7 mutants develop more nodules, indicating strigolactones negatively regulate nodulation in M. truncatula.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing strigolactone biosynthesis may improve AMF recruitment, but could have opposite effects on nodulation and potentially alter other developmental traits.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Processes carotenoid-derived precursors in the strigolactone biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulate AMF hyphal branching and fungal LCO production, promoting mycorrhization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CCD8a reduces AMF colonization, supporting a requirement for strigolactone biosynthesis in AMF association
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ccd8a mutants show reduced R. irregularis colonization. 2. This indicates the strigolactone biosynthetic pathway positively regulates AMF establishment. 3. Strigolactone biosynthesis has distinct roles from D14 signaling in the plant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tnt1 mutant analysis; AMF colonization assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper mentions two CCD8 homologs in M. truncatula; CCD8a was functionally tested.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ccd8a mutants develop more nodules, indicating negative regulation of nodulation by strigolactone pathway output.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target to modulate AMF recruitment through enhanced strigolactone production, with caution for nodulation and other strigolactone-dependent traits.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Iron-containing isomerase family associated with strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Isomerized carotenoid precursor(s) for strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an early carotenoid isomerization step required for strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulated step suggested but not directly established here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulate AMF fungal responses and support symbiosis establishment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NSP1 is required for root expression of D27, linking NSP transcriptional control to strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D27 is among the apocarotenoid/strigolactone biosynthetic genes under NSP regulation. 2. In M. truncatula nsp1 mutants, root expression of D27 is lost. 3. NSP1 and NSP2 together most strongly activate D27 and related strigolactone biosynthesis genes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root expression induced in nutrient starvation and dependent on NSP1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: D27 was used as a representative direct readout of NSP control over apocarotenoid biosynthesis rather than as a mutant phenotype centerpiece.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMtD27 promoter used as GUS transcriptional reporter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker and engineering node for enhancing strigolactone output downstream of NSP regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root exudates/rhizosphere for strigolactones; root surface for LCO perception
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strigolactones promote AMF engagement and fungal LCO production; LCO perception activates symbiosis signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nutrient starvation-induced strigolactone biosynthesis, LCO perception, and AMF colonization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSP1 family genes are induced by nutrient starvation in M. truncatula and barley, with HvNSP1a showing strong induction. 2. nsp1 mutants have impaired nutrient starvation-induced LCO calcium responses and reduced AMF colonization. 3. NSP1 is required for expression of apocarotenoid biosynthetic genes such as D27. 4. In barley, nsp1 mutants lose P starvation-induced strigolactone production.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; AMF colonization assays; calcium oscillation assays; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; strigolactone quantification; overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates strigolactone/apocarotenoid biosynthetic genes including D27; contributes to induction of RLK10 and symbiosis signaling genes under nutrient starvation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential component of the nutrient-starvation regulatory module enabling metabolic and signaling preparation for AMF symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen starvation; phosphorus starvation; PHR2 (indirectly discussed for P starvation pathway)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root expression induced under N and/or P starvation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In M. truncatula, NSP1 overexpression alone is insufficient to strongly activate strigolactone biosynthetic genes, indicating combinatorial action with NSP2.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression constructs used; specific promoter not stated in main text
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering NSP1 with NSP2 may enhance AMF colonization and nutrient foraging, but NSP1 alone may be insufficient for strong effects.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root exudates/rhizosphere for strigolactones; root surface for LCO perception
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Coordinates AMF engagement by promoting strigolactone biosynthesis and enabling LCO receptor expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major positive regulator of nutrient starvation-induced strigolactone biosynthesis, D14L/SMAX1 signaling, RLK10 expression, LCO recognition, and AMF colonization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSP2 expression is nutrient responsive and functionally required for nutrient regulation of LCO-induced calcium oscillations and AMF colonization. 2. NSP2 misregulation is sufficient to partially overcome phosphate suppression of mycorrhization in both M. truncatula and barley. 3. NSP2 overexpression increases strigolactone levels, including under high phosphate where NSP1 does not. 4. NSP2 acts upstream of the D14L/SMAX1 module and promotes RLK10, SYMRK, and CYCLOPS expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants; overexpression of native and miR171h-resistant NSP2; calcium oscillation assays; AMF colonization assays; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; strigolactone quantification; genetic epistasis with d14/d14l/smax1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation upstream of D14L/SMAX1 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates strigolactone/apocarotenoid biosynthetic genes; promotes RLK10, SYMRK, and CYCLOPS expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: D27 and other strigolactone biosynthetic genes; RLK10; SYMRK; CYCLOPS
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Apocarotenoid biosynthesis; LCO receptor expression; symbiosis signaling competence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Dominant regulator that can override phosphate repression of AMF colonization and LCO perception
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen starvation; phosphorus starvation; PHR2; miR171h
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts in a nutrient-responsive feedforward module coordinating strigolactone production and LCO perception
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely functions combinatorially with NSP1; genetically upstream of D14L/SMAX1 signaling module
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root expression induced by nutrient starvation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots under N/P starvation; overexpression active under nutrient-replete conditions in transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen starvation; phosphorus starvation; phosphate-replete versus phosphate-limited conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In M. truncatula, miR171h suppresses NSP2; only miR171h-resistant NSP2 strongly promotes AMF colonization under repressive P. Legume NSP2 and NSP2L are partially redundant for AMF colonization.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misregulation may alter broader nutrient starvation transcriptomes and apocarotenoid metabolism; native overexpression effect can be limited by miR171h in M. truncatula.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression constructs used; specific promoter not stated in main text
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for enhancing AMF colonization under phosphate-sufficient agriculture, especially using miRNA-resistant forms or coordinated deployment with NSP1 and receptor targets.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Post-transcriptional regulation by miR171h represses NSP2; miR171h-resistant NSP2 enhances AMF colonization

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Medicago truncatula; Hordeum vulgare
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Medicago truncatula; Hordeum vulgare
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enables plant perception of AMF-associated LCOs and supports AMF colonization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for LCO responsiveness, RLK10 induction, and AMF colonization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In rice, d14l mutants abolish LCO responsiveness and attenuate CO responsiveness. 2. In M. truncatula and barley, d14l mutants show reduced AMF colonization. 3. D14L is required for RLK10 induction by nutrient starvation, 5DS, and karrikins. 4. NSP2-mediated enhancement of AMF colonization depends on D14L, placing D14L downstream of NSP2.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; calcium oscillation assays; RT-qPCR; AMF colonization assays; pharmacological treatments; genetic epistasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Signal perception leading to derepression via SMAX1 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of RLK10 and contributes to SYMRK/CYCLOPS activation indirectly through SMAX1 removal
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: LCO receptor expression and symbiotic signal competence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential receptor-like regulator enabling nutrient-controlled activation of LCO perception
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Karrikins; likely endogenous karrikin-like/apocarotenoid-derived ligand; 5-deoxystrigol-dependent effect observed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study suggests D14L, not D14, is the relevant receptor for internal signaling supporting AMF establishment.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering cereal and legume AMF competence without directly altering broad phosphate-starvation regulators.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant competence for symbiotic LCO perception and AMF colonization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a suppressor of AMF colonization and symbiosis-related gene expression; loss enhances colonization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. smax1 mutants in M. truncatula show enhanced AMF colonization. 2. The nsp2/nsp2l/smax1 triple mutant resembles smax1 rather than nsp2/nsp2l, placing SMAX1 downstream of NSP2. 3. Prior evidence cited and current data support SMAX1 as the suppressor removed by D14L signaling to permit symbiosis gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; genetic epistasis in triple mutant; AMF colonization assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative repression relieved by D14L signaling-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses RLK10/NFR5 and symbiosis signaling genes such as SYMRK and CYCLOPS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key suppressor whose removal is necessary for nutrient-regulated LCO perception and AMF colonization
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because the Results section uses genetic epistasis to position NSP2 upstream of SMAX1 in the pathway controlling AMF colonization.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced colonization in smax1 may reflect broad derepression of signaling outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial suppression of SMAX1 activity could enhance AMF competence, but broad signaling derepression may require caution.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports signaling competence for AMF symbiosis via karrikin/strigolactone-related signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. max2 mutants show reduced R. irregularis colonization. 2. MAX2/D3 acts as a positive regulator of AMF colonization in the signaling module shared with D14L. 3. The data support karrikin-like signaling, rather than D14 signaling, as important for AMF establishment.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tnt1 mutant analysis; AMF colonization assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Signal transduction via SCF-type ubiquitin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely promotes symbiosis gene expression through degradation of SMAX1-like repressors
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required component of the positive signaling branch enabling AMF colonization
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: SCF E3 ubiquitin ligase signaling module
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not as central as NSP1/NSP2/D14L, but experimentally validated in Results as part of the signaling module controlling AMF colonization.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for tuning D14L-SMAX1 signaling, though broad hormone/signaling pleiotropy should be considered.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Strigolactone signaling output affecting AMF and nodulation traits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Canonical strigolactone perception pathway; here shown not required for positive AMF regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: D14 is not required for AMF colonization and may negatively affect it; D14 signaling represses nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. d14 mutants show enhanced or not reduced AMF colonization, indicating plant D14 perception is not required for AMF symbiosis. 2. NSP2-mediated promotion of AMF colonization does not require D14. 3. D14-mediated strigolactone signaling negatively regulates nodulation in M. truncatula.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tnt1 mutant analysis; AMF colonization assays; genetic epistasis with NSP2 overexpression; nodulation assay
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Strigolactone signaling distinct from AMF-promoting D14L pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important negative/control comparator demonstrating that strigolactone biosynthesis and D14L signaling, not D14 signaling, underpin AMF promotion
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because Results explicitly distinguish D14 from D14L to define the causal signaling route.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: D14 loss may alter nodulation and other strigolactone-regulated developmental traits.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid relying on D14 signaling to enhance AMF; separation from D14L pathway may help engineer symbiosis more specifically.
